witnesses.description: A bijection between two explicit three-element finite sets.
A proper-subset injection from two elements into a three-element set.

checks.claim: The listed three-element finite sets have equal cardinality by the displayed bijection.
checks.notes: The validator checks the graph is total, single-valued, injective, and surjective.

checks.claim: The listed two-element proper subset injects into the listed three-element set without being surjective.
checks.notes: The validator checks proper inclusion, totality, single-valuedness, injectivity, and non-surjectivity.

checks.claim: There exists an injective function from a four-element set to a three-element set.
checks.notes: The validator enumerates all 81 functions and confirms none is injective.

checks.claim: There exists a surjective function from a two-element set onto a three-element set.
checks.notes: The validator enumerates all 9 functions and confirms none is surjective.

checks.claim: There is no surjection from the natural numbers onto the powerset of the natural numbers.

data.future_checker: Lean module with no sorryAx dependency

checks.notes: This infinite cardinality theorem is recorded as a theorem-prover target, not as finite evidence.
